sql_shape: LEFT JOIN entity_classification_history {ec} ON {ec}.entity_id = {h}.entity_id AND {ec}.valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31'
entity_type_filter_applied: implicit (h is holdings_v2 keyed on manager institution)
notes: holdings_v2.entity_id always references manager institution eid (13F filer) — funds are never the join target. Closing fund-typed ECH leaves this path unaffected.

sql_shape: LEFT JOIN entity_current {ec} ON {ec}.entity_id = {h}.entity_id (or via entity_identifiers cik bridge)
notes: Used by Tier 4 holdings queries. Same reasoning as classification_join — joins on manager institution eid.

sql_shape: SELECT entity_id, display_name, entity_type, classification FROM entity_current WHERE entity_id = rollup_entity_id AND display_name ILIKE ? ORDER BY display_name LIMIT 20
entity_type_filter_applied: none (returns any entity_type that is self-rollup)
notes: Self-rollup filter mostly excludes funds (funds typically have a fund_sponsor parent). Fund eids that ARE self-rollup will display NULL classification after close — cosmetic only in the type-ahead dropdown.

sql_shape: SELECT entity_id, display_name, entity_type, classification, rollup_entity_id FROM entity_current WHERE entity_id = ?
migration_target: if ec.entity_type='fund' read classification from fund_universe.fund_strategy by series_id
notes: Single-eid lookup powering the node-detail panel. UI shows classification value. Funds will show NULL after close. Migration: in get_entity_by_id, when entity_type='fund', resolve classification via series_id → fund_universe.fund_strategy.

sql_shape: JOIN entity_current ec ON ec.entity_id = d.entity_id (reads ec.display_name only)
notes: Only ec.display_name and ec.entity_id read. No classification dependency.

sql_shape: JOIN entity_current ec ON ec.entity_id = er.child_entity_id (reads ec.display_name and ec.entity_id)
notes: ec.classification not read. Returns NAV from fund_universe directly. No dependency on ECH.

sql_shape: JOIN entity_current ec ON ec.entity_id = er.child_entity_id (ec.display_name only)
JOIN entity_current ec ON ec.entity_id = er.child_entity_id (ec.display_name only) + LEFT JOIN entity_identifiers series_id
notes: display_name read only — funds are children but classification value not consumed.

sql_shape: SELECT DISTINCT ec.entity_id, ec.display_name, ec.classification FROM entity_relationships er JOIN entity_current ec ON ec.entity_id = er.parent_entity_id WHERE er.relationship_type = 'sub_adviser'
entity_type_filter_applied: institution_only (parent of sub_adviser edge is institution)
notes: Sub-adviser parents are institutions. Funds are never the parent of a sub_adviser edge.

reader_path: scripts/admin_bp.py:1059-1064 (CSV reclassify is_activist branch — read prior class)
sql_shape: SELECT classification FROM entity_classification_history WHERE entity_id = ? ORDER BY valid_from DESC LIMIT 1
notes: Reads prior classification to preserve it when flipping is_activist. After PR-C the SELECT returns no row for fund eids → fallback to 'unknown'. But the writer side will be gated to reject fund eids first (see writer disable PR), so this read becomes unreachable for funds.

sql_shape: LEFT JOIN entity_classification_history AS ech ON e.entity_id = ech.entity_id AND ech.valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31'
notes: Fixture builder rebuilds entity_current as a VIEW in the test DB. LEFT JOIN handles missing rows.

sql_shape: SELECT 1 FROM entity_classification_history WHERE entity_id = ? AND valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31'
notes: Gate raises 'no_active_classification_history_row' when requires_classification=True. All live callers (load_13f_v2, load_nport, load_13dg) pass requires_classification=False. Only retired validate_nport.py uses True.

sql_shape: FULL OUTER JOIN of stg_active vs prod_active filtered on valid_to=DATE '9999-12-31'
notes: Reporting only. Will show 13220 'deleted' on the PR-C close cycle — expected and informational.

sql_shape: LEFT JOIN entity_classification_history ech ON e.entity_id = ech.entity_id AND ech.valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31' WHERE ech.entity_id IS NULL
notes: This is the write site itself. After Phase 1 disables fund writes, this loop must be gated to skip entity_type='fund' so it doesn't try to default-classify funds as 'unknown'.

sql_shape: SELECT classification FROM entity_classification_history WHERE entity_id = ? AND valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31'
notes: Used in SIC reclassification path. Will be gated alongside the writer (rare for funds; only fund-with-CIK + unknown edge case).

sql_shape: JOIN entity_classification_history ech ON ech.entity_id = e.entity_id AND ech.valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31' WHERE ech.classification = 'unknown' AND e.created_source != 'PARENT_SEEDS'
notes: Picks unknown-classified CIKs for SEC EDGAR resolution. After close fund-typed eids drop out (no ECH row → INNER JOIN excludes them). Intended behavior — funds should not be in this worker queue.

sql_shape: SELECT 1 FROM entity_classification_history WHERE entity_id=? AND valid_to = DATE '9999-12-31'
notes: Idempotency guard — checks if ECH row exists before inserting. After Phase 1 disables this writer for fund entities the guard becomes irrelevant on the disabled branch.
